perturbation.original_text: 13.4 Insurance. Each Party, at its own expense, shall maintain comprehensive general liability, product liability and other appropriate insurance for the activities such Party undertakes pursuant to this Agreement, from reputable and financially secure insurance carriers in a form and at levels consistent with sound business practice and adequate in light of its obligations under this Agreement. Each Party shall provide a certificate of insurance (or evidence of self-insurance) evidencing such coverage to the other Party upon request. Such insurance will not create a limit to a Party's liability with respect to its indemnification obligations under this Article 13 or otherwise. This Section 13.4 will survive expiration or termination of this Agreement for the period in which the Licensed Product is being Commercialized by or on behalf of Vyera plus six (6) years. Each Party shall provide the other Party with prompt written notice of any cancellation, non-renewal or material change in such insurance that could materially adversely affect the rights of the other Party hereunder, and shall provide such notice within thirty (30) days after any such cancellation, non-renewal or material change.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.4 Insurance. Each Party, at its own expense, shall maintain general liability insurance for the activities such Party undertakes pursuant to this Agreement. Each Party shall provide a certificate of insurance (or evidence of self-insurance) evidencing such coverage to the other Party upon request. This Section 13.4 will survive expiration or termination of this Agreement for the period in which the Licensed Product is being Commercialized by or on behalf of Vyera plus six (6) years. Each Party will try to provide the other Party with written notice of any cancellation, non-renewal or material change in such insurance.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'comprehensive' and 'product liability' requirements, as well as weakening the obligation to provide notice of changes, this modified text creates a legal risk. The original text mandates specific insurance coverage, whereas the modified text only requires general liability insurance and weakens the duty to notify of changes.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY GBL § 391-g requires businesses to maintain adequate insurance and proper notification of insurance changes. By removing the 'comprehensive' and 'product liability' insurance requirements and weakening the notification obligation, the modified text introduces a contradiction of this law, exposing the parties to legal liability due to inadequate coverage and insufficient notice.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: New York General Business Law § 391-G (2022) - Rental of Motor Vehicles; Discrimination on the Basis of Age Prohibited. :: 2022 New York Laws :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of the New York Laws 2024 N.Y. Laws 2023 N.Y. Laws 2022 N.Y. Laws (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 New York Laws GBS - General Business Article 26 - Miscellaneous 391-G - Rental of Motor Vehicles; Discrimination on the Basis of Age Prohibited. Universal Citation: NY Gen Bus L § 391-G (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next § 391-g. Rental of motor vehicles; discrimination on the basis of age prohibited. 1. It shall be unlawful for any person, firm, partnership, association or corporation engaged in the business of renting motor vehicles to refuse to rent such vehicle to any person eighteen years of age or older solely on the basis of age provided that insurance coverage for persons of such age is available. Any actual extra cost for insurance related to the age of the person renting such motor vehicle may be passed on to such person. 2. A knowing violation of this section shall be punishable by a fine not to exceed five hundred dollars. Previous Next Disclaimer: These codes may not be the most recent version. perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates 'comprehensive general liability, product liability,' while the changed version only requires 'general liability insurance.' This weakens the insurance requirement. The original also requires 'prompt written notice' of changes in insurance, while the changed version states the party will 'try to provide written notice,' weakening the notification obligation. The Perturbation Explanation says that the original text mandates specific insurance coverage, whereas the modified text only requires general liability insurance and weakens the duty to notify of changes.

perturbation.original_text:
All notices and other communications given or made pursuant hereto shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given on the date delivered, if delivered personally, or on the next Business Day after being sent by reputable international overnight courier (with delivery tracking provided, signature required and delivery prepaid), in each case, to the Parties at the following addresses, each as may be specified below (or at such other address for a Party as shall be specified by notice given in accordance with this Section 14.1). If to Vyera:

600 Third Avenue, 10t h Floor
New York, NY 10016

perturbation.changed_text:
All notices and other communications given or made pursuant hereto shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given on the date delivered, if delivered personally, or on the next Business Day after being sent by courier, in each case, to the Parties at the following addresses, each as may be specified below (or at such other address for a Party as shall be specified by notice given in accordance with this Section 14.1). If to Vyera:
Vyera Pharmaceuticals, LLC
600 Third Avenue, 10t h Floor
New York, NY 10016
Attention: Unknown Department
Email: [***]
perturbation.explanation: By removing specifics like 'reputable international overnight courier (with delivery tracking provided, signature required and delivery prepaid)' and changing 'Legal Department' to 'Unknown Department', the modified text introduces ambiguity and reduces the reliability of the notice provisions. This creates legal risk because it becomes harder to prove that notices were properly served, and the removal of 'Legal Department' can mean the recipient may not have the legal expertise to address it.
perturbation.contradicted_law: New York Civil Practice Law and Rules (CPLR) § 2103
perturbation.law_explanation: NY CPLR § 2103 outlines the requirements for serving papers in legal proceedings. By weakening the notice provisions, there's a contradiction with the requirements for proper service of legal documents, which mandates clear and reliable methods for delivering notices.

perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the notice provisions by removing specifics like 'reputable international overnight courier (with delivery tracking provided, signature required and delivery prepaid)' and changing 'Legal Department' to 'Unknown Department'. This creates a contradiction with NY CPLR § 2103, which outlines the requirements for serving papers in legal proceedings, as the changes introduce ambiguity and reduce the reliability of the notice provisions. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing NY CPLR § 2103, discusses service of papers. The original text provided a clear and reliable method for delivering notices. The modified text, by removing key details, could make it more difficult to prove proper service, creating ambiguity and potential non-compliance with legal requirements for proper notice.

perturbation.original_text: 14.12 Entire Agreement. This Agreement, including the Attachments hereto, sets forth the complete, final and exclusive agreement and all the covenants, promises, agreements, warranties, representations, conditions and understandings between the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes, as of the Effective Date, all prior and contemporaneous agreements and understandings between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof; including the Mutual Confidential Disclosure Agreement between the Parties dated as of January 31, 2019. There are no covenants, promises, agreements, warranties, representations, conditions or understandings, either oral or written, between the Parties other than as are set forth herein and therein. No subsequent alteration, amendment, change or addition to this Agreement shall be binding upon the Parties unless reduced to writing and signed by an authorized officer of each Party. In the event of any inconsistency between the body of this Agreement and either any Attachments to this Agreement or any subsequent agreements ancillary to this Agreement, unless otherwise express stated to the contrary in such Attachment or ancillary agreement, the terms contained in this Agreement shall control.
perturbation.changed_text: 14.12 Entire Agreement. This Agreement sets forth the agreement and all the covenants, promises, agreements, warranties, representations, conditions and understandings between the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes, as of the Effective Date, all prior agreements and understandings between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. There are no covenants, promises, agreements, warranties, representations, conditions or understandings, either oral or written, between the Parties other than as are set forth herein. No subsequent alteration, amendment, change or addition to this Agreement shall be binding upon the Parties unless reduced to writing. In the event of any inconsistency between the body of this Agreement and any Attachments to this Agreement or any subsequent agreements ancillary to this Agreement, the terms contained in this Agreement shall control.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'complete, final and exclusive agreement' and 'signed by an authorized officer of each Party', this modified text introduces potential loopholes. The absence of 'complete, final and exclusive' suggests other agreements might exist. Removing the requirement for authorized officer signatures weakens the amendment process, potentially making the contract non-compliant with corporate law.
perturbation.law_explanation: 8 Del. C. § 104 requires that amendments to a corporation's charter be duly adopted and executed by authorized officers. Removing the requirement for authorized officer signatures in the amended text creates a contradiction with the state's corporate governance laws.

perturbation.justification: The modified text omits the requirement for amendments to be 'signed by an authorized officer of each Party.' The Law Explanation argues this contradicts 8 Del. C. § 104, which requires corporate charter amendments to be executed by authorized officers. Scraped Law Snippet 1 includes a reference to § 104 within Title 8, Chapter 1 of the Delaware Code, thus supporting the relevance of Delaware corporate law. This omission weakens the amendment process, potentially creating a contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation.
